title: Panjab University Results 2026 Key Highlights

detail.title: Panjab University Results 2026 Key Highlights
detail.short_info: Panjab University has officially declared the UG and Diploma Results 2026 for ADCA, B.Sc Data Analytics, and B.P.Ed semester exams. Students can check their result status online through the official Panjab University exam portal at results.puexam.in.

content.text: Panjab University Result 2026 has been officially released for various UG and diploma courses including Advanced Diploma in Computer Application (ADCA), B.Sc Data Analytics (FYUP), and B.P.Ed 7th Semester examinations held in December 2025. Students who appeared for these exams can now check their results online.

Panjab University Result 2026 has been released online on the official website - https://results.puexam.in/. Candidates can access their results by entering their roll number or login credentials through the official result portal. It is advised that students carefully verify all the details mentioned in their scorecard and contact the university authorities in case of any discrepancies.

Panjab University has released the latest semester examination results for multiple UG and diploma courses for the December 2025 session. Students can now access their results online through the official portal using their roll number.

Panjab University has activated the direct result links for ADCA, B.Sc Data Analytics, and B.P.Ed semester examinations. Students can check and download their results instantly through the official portal using the links provided below.

links.label: Advanced Diploma in Computer Application 1st Semester Examination December 2025
B.Sc Data Analytics (FYUP) 1st Semester Examination December 2025

sections.heading: How to Check Panjab University Result 2026

content.text: Follow these simple steps to check your Panjab University result online:

items: Click on the relevant course or semester result link
Enter your roll number or required login details
Your result will be displayed on the screen
Download and take a printout for future reference
